name: frontend-skills
description: React/TypeScript frontend + Go backend enforcement harness. 92 wired hooks (101 scripts) enforce patterns on every edit. Green != done: `test-warning-check` (local Bash PostToolUse) + `ci-warning-audit` (Stop, scans `gh run view --log`) block passing tests with DeprecationWarning / React `act()` / unhandled rejection / `@ts-ignore` noise. 71 skills from TDD through CI-green PR, including /diagnose feedback-loop-first 6-phase debugger + /triage multi-tracker (gh + acli) state machine + /steelman anti-sycophancy guard + /snyk-ux-security JS+Go per-path vuln sweep (exploitability-triage first gate, top-level-first upgrade ladder, React 18 pin, `govulncheck` for Go). 21 LLM failure modes (7 Karpathy single-agent + 14 MAST multi-agent, Cemri et al. NeurIPS 2025), OWASP + STRIDE, Core Web Vitals gate, bun.lock + yarn.lock parity, worktree isolation + branch safety, MCP ban with CLI redirect (~20x savings), agent-browser integration (~91% token reduction). 9 agents (3 plan-review hats + karpathy reference), 5 routines. Single-source manifest codegen. Opus 4.7 tuned. POSIX-friendly.

interface.shortDescription: React/TypeScript + Go enforcement harness — 92 hooks, 101 scripts, 71 skills, 9 agents, 5 routines
interface.longDescription: React/TypeScript frontend + Go backend enforcement harness. 92 wired hooks across 101 scripts enforce patterns on every edit, including accessibility, Tailwind, React Compiler, Zustand, TanStack Router, connect-query, env validation, strict TypeScript, unhappy paths, security, warnings, CI, and PR feedback completeness. 71 skills guide the development lifecycle from TDD through CI-green PR, including /diagnose, /triage, /steelman, /snyk-ux-security, and grill-with-docs/ADR workflows. 9 agents support review and planning hats. 5 routines automate recurring workflows. Codex packaging maps every supported equivalent lifecycle event, including PermissionRequest and PostToolUse failure handling, while Claude packaging keeps the full hook surface.
defaultPrompt: Set up the frontend development lifecycle
Run the full quality gate on my changes
Review this PR and fix all findings

x-changelog.4.12.0: 2026-06-26 -- Upstream the 8-hat `/review` skill and its dependency skills from the personal fork (`malinskibeniamin/skills`). Replaces the prior 21-line `/review` stub with the full diff-review-from-a-fixed-point workflow: 8 parallel review hats (ponytail, thermo-nuclear, resilience, regular Standards+Spec, adversarial, visual, test-perf, security-privacy-triage) via general-purpose subagents or `/swarm`, merged + deduped by root cause, a PR value gate with a `/steelman` fallback, and auto-posted P0-P3 inline PR comments with a comment-ready fallback. Adds the five skills `/review` invokes that were missing from ui-harness: `/deslop`, `/ponytail-review`, `/resilience-review`, `/thermo-nuclear-code-quality-review`, `/swarm` (`/visual-review`, `/steelman`, `/grill-me` already present). New evals: test-review (26 checks), test-thermo-nuclear-code-quality-review, test-swarm (deslop and resilience-review ship without evals here -- their fork evals assert harness-wide integration that is out of scope). Skills 66 -> 71.
x-changelog.4.11.0: 2026-05-26 -- Add `/visual-review` skill for browser-based frontend QA before PRs. The workflow runs standalone and is wired into `/go`, `/commit-push`, `/commit-push-pr`, self-reviewer, and code-reviewer for frontend diffs, requiring visual review evidence or an explicit skip reason. Ships platform/environment fingerprinting (browser, user agent, platform, viewport, visualViewport, DPR, media preferences, locale/direction), a platform risk map, Web Weekly-informed visual/a11y/perf checks, and 79 eval assertions. Skills 65 -> 66.
x-changelog.4.10.6: 2026-05-25 -- Codex marketplace install fix: expose root plugin through root plugin path so Codex marketplace source path is a plugin directory while retaining root-relative plugin packaging.
x-changelog.4.10.5: 2026-05-25 -- Vendor remaining mattpocock/skills into repo, caveman-compress new skill docs, add vendoring evals, and fix detached-HEAD branch-safety eval coverage.
x-changelog.4.10.4: 2026-05-25 -- Complete mattpocock/skills May 18-20 sync: add /improve-codebase-architecture HTML-report workflow + HTML-REPORT.md, redaction guardrails in /handoff, and glossary-only CONTEXT-FORMAT cleanup; evals expanded.
x-changelog.4.10.3: 2026-05-21 -- Add /handoff skill for compact cross-session and cross-agent continuation briefs. Registers the skill in plugin packaging, documents the workflow, and adds eval coverage. Skills 51 -> 52.
x-changelog.4.10.2: 2026-05-06 -- Codex hook parity pass: map Claude PostToolUseFailure into Codex PostToolUse, add a Codex PermissionRequest adapter that reuses approval-safe Bash/MCP deny guardrails, and regenerate Codex hooks so the plugin uses every supported Codex lifecycle event with an equivalent harness behavior.
x-changelog.4.10.1: 2026-05-06 -- Codex marketplace packaging fix: point Codex plugin hooks at a Codex-supported lifecycle file, regenerate .codex/hooks.json from the manifest, refresh stale install-surface copy (92 hooks / 100 scripts / 51 skills / 9 agents / 5 routines), and bump marketplace metadata for upgrade detection.
x-changelog.4.10.0: 2026-04-29 -- Sync mattpocock/skills (Apr 17 -> Apr 29). New `/diagnose` skill ported verbatim: feedback-loop-first 6-phase debug discipline (build loop -> reproduce -> 3-5 ranked falsifiable hypotheses -> instrument with `[DEBUG-prefix]` cleanup grep -> fix at correct seam + regression test -> cleanup + post-mortem hand-off to `/improve-codebase-architecture`); 10 ranked Phase-1 strategies; ships `scripts/hitl-loop.template.sh`. Merged `github-triage` + `triage-issue` into single `/triage` (multi-tracker: `tracker-github.md` via `gh issue`, `tracker-jira.md` via `acli jira workitem`); bug-investigation + TDD-fix-plan absorbed as sub-mode; 5 canonical state roles map to project-specific labels/statuses; AI-disclaimer required on every triage comment. Vague-prose domain-glossary + ADR awareness added to `/tdd`, `/improve-codebase-architecture`, `/zoom-out`, `/triage` -- soft-dep, degrades gracefully when no `CONTEXT.md`. Upstream content merged while preserving 3-hat fan-out: `improve-codebase-architecture/LANGUAGE.md` (canonical vocab + deletion test); `tdd/tests.md` (good vs bad tests); `domain-model/ADR-FORMAT.md` 'what qualifies' enriched. Skills 48 -> 51. +96 eval checks (test-triage, test-diagnose, test-domain-model-merge); test-tdd patched. Skipped per direction: caveman, git-guardrails, to-prd/to-issues, setup skill explicit pointer.
x-changelog.4.9.0: 2026-04-23 -- Green != done warnings gate (+2 hooks, 89->91 wired / 98->100 scripts). `test-warning-check.sh` (PostToolUse Bash) scans passing `vitest`/`bun test`/`playwright`/`tsgo`/`biome`/`lint` output for curated warning patterns: Node `DeprecationWarning` + `ExperimentalWarning` + `(node:...) XxxWarning`, React `act()`/missing-key/DOM-nesting/prop-type/bad-setstate/non-boolean-attr, unhandled promise rejection / unhandled error, vitest `stderr |` during test, skipped-test counts, `@ts-expect-error` / `@ts-ignore`, `npm WARN deprecated`. Per-finding escape `// allow: test-warning` in test file with reason; env `TEST_WARNINGS_ALLOW=1`. 3x same-kind streak escalates via systemMessage. `ci-warning-audit.sh` (Stop) fetches `gh run view <id> --log` on green PR CI (per-SHA cache, 45s timeout), greps same pattern, warns with file:line sample + fetch-full command. Gates: feature branch, session touched code, PR exists, all checks SUCCESS (pending/fail defer to lifecycle-stop). Disable via `CI_WARNING_AUDIT=0`. CLAUDE.md Tests section adds green-not-done rule. tdd/SKILL.md `When Done` adds zero-warnings local + CI log audit checkboxes. Why: tests can be green while runtime spews deprecations / logs errors / leaks unhandled rejections -- noise rots into real bugs when the libs actually remove the API. Agent must clean before calling it done.
x-changelog.4.8.0: 2026-04-22 -- /steelman anti-sycophancy skill (opt-in only). New skill at steelman/SKILL.md argues strongest opposite case with repo evidence on user assertions -- factual/causal/architectural claim types only (preference/goal/scope declined). Procedure: identify claim -> fan-out evidence gather (grep, read, run) -> write counter-argument with file:line refs -> verdict (confirmed | contradicted | mixed). User-invoked via `/steelman` slash only. **No ambient CLAUDE.md rule, no automatic hook fire, no telemetry log script** -- plugin consumers see zero behavior change unless they type `/steelman`. Ambient premise-check directive + jsonl telemetry were considered and held back pending real-world feedback on the opt-in surface; re-evaluate once /steelman fire-rate data exists from dogfooding. Why: LLM sycophancy risk when the user asserts something wrong and the model agrees by default -- /steelman forces the strongest counter-case with repo evidence before proceeding. Skills 47 -> 48 (registers the skill file that was previously on disk but unlisted).
